title: MD
biography: Joseph Lister was a British surgeon who pioneered antiseptic surgery by introducing carbolic acid sterilization of instruments and wound dressings in the 1860s. His techniques dramatically reduced surgical mortality from infection and laid the foundation for modern sterile surgical practice.
aiSummary: Joseph Lister was the Father of Antiseptic Surgery who introduced carbolic acid sterilization, dramatically reducing surgical mortality. Based in London, United Kingdom.

timeline.title: Introduced antiseptic surgery
timeline.description: Applied carbolic acid to compound fracture wounds at Glasgow Royal Infirmary

timeline.title: Published antiseptic principle
timeline.description: Published 'On the Antiseptic Principle in the Practice of Surgery' in The Lancet

timeline.title: Professor at King's College Hospital
timeline.description: Moved to London, where his antiseptic methods gained wider acceptance
